title: The Eternal Cylinder
anchor_summary: Single-player alien-world exploration adventure built around guiding and growing a small creature family, unlocking mutations for traversal and survival, solving environmental puzzles, and adapting to a hostile ecosystem rather than focusing on conventional combat.

similar_games.title: Ancestors: The Humankind Odyssey
similar_games.why_similar: Survival-exploration loop centered on evolving a vulnerable group, expanding abilities, and navigating a dangerous world through adaptation rather than direct combat.

similar_games.title: Rain World
similar_games.why_similar: Harsh ecosystem-driven survival where a fragile creature explores, evades predators, and learns the world through movement, observation, and environmental hazards.

similar_games.title: Pikmin 4
similar_games.why_similar: Exploration and puzzle progression built around leading a small creature group with different abilities to overcome traversal challenges and hazards.

similar_games.title: Journey to the Savage Planet
similar_games.why_similar: Alien-planet exploration focused on scanning wildlife, unlocking traversal upgrades, and pushing into new biomes through curiosity and environmental problem-solving.

similar_games.title: Spore
similar_games.why_similar: Best-known comp for creature evolution and body-based ability changes, even if its structure is broader and more sandbox-like than The Eternal Cylinder.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently depict a single-player, third-person 3D open-world survival adventure centered on exploring an alien planet, evading a world-destroying cylinder, solving environmental puzzles, and adapting a herd via mutations that unlock traversal and survival abilities. The core loop is exploration, survival under pressure, puzzle-solving, and story progression rather than shooter combat or RPG party tactics. Repeated mentions of a procedurally generated ecosystem, emergent gameplay, real-time world destruction, rescuing and growing the herd, and mutation-based adaptation strongly support exploration_survival_adventure.

title: Juno: New Origins
anchor_summary: Physics-heavy aerospace/vehicle construction sandbox focused on designing custom craft, testing them in a simulated 3D environment, and progressing through contracts, milestones, and tech unlocks.

similar_games.title: Kerbal Space Program
similar_games.why_similar: The clearest comp: build rockets and spacecraft, manage staging and orbital mechanics, and progress through missions in a physics-driven space program sandbox.

similar_games.title: SimplePlanes
similar_games.why_similar: Also from Jundroo, with the same core loop of deeply customizable vehicle building, flight testing, tuning parts, and optional automation/programming.

similar_games.title: Stormworks: Build and Rescue
similar_games.why_similar: Engineering sandbox built around assembling vehicles from modular parts, testing realistic behavior, and using logic/programming systems to automate craft.

similar_games.title: Trailmakers
similar_games.why_similar: Creative build-and-drive/fly sandbox centered on making custom vehicles and iterating on handling and performance, though with lighter physics and progression.

similar_games.title: Space Engineers
similar_games.why_similar: Sandbox focused on constructing functional ships and vehicles, then piloting and refining them in a simulation-heavy environment with strong engineering emphasis.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently define Juno: New Origins as a 3D aerospace sandbox centered on constructing and testing rockets, planes, cars, and planets with realistic physics. The core loop is design/build/test/iterate, with deep vehicle customization, orbital mechanics, automation scripting, persistent sandbox play, and a career layer of contracts, milestones, money, and tech-tree unlocks. There is no combat emphasis; the challenge comes from simulation realism and engineering optimization. This strongly fits engineering_sandbox_sim.

title: Insurgency
anchor_summary: Hardcore tactical FPS centered on lethal close-quarters gunfights, objective-based PvP, and mission-based co-op with minimal HUD, realistic weapon handling, and teamwork-heavy class/squad play.

similar_games.title: Insurgency: Sandstorm
similar_games.why_similar: Direct follow-up with the same lethal tactical gunplay, objective modes, squad roles, and co-op checkpoint structure.

similar_games.title: Day of Infamy
similar_games.why_similar: Built on a very similar class-based, low-HUD, one-life/limited-respawn tactical FPS framework, just shifted to a WWII setting.

similar_games.title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam
similar_games.why_similar: Another unforgiving objective shooter focused on realistic weapon lethality, territory control, asymmetry, and team coordination.

similar_games.title: Squad
similar_games.why_similar: Shares realism, communication, squad roles, and objective-driven combat, though on a larger-scale milsim battlefield.

similar_games.title: Red Orchestra 2: Heroes of Stalingrad
similar_games.why_similar: Similarly emphasizes deadly gunplay, suppression, minimal handholding, and teamwork in structured objective matches.

title: Sons of Valhalla
anchor_summary: Side-scrolling action-strategy hybrid focused on building strongholds, recruiting and upgrading a warband, issuing battlefield commands, and personally fighting alongside troops across a roguelite-style campaign map.

similar_games.title: Kingdom Two Crowns
similar_games.why_similar: Closest recognizable comp for the side-scrolling kingdom-building loop, resource management, expanding outposts, and personally riding with troops as battles unfold in real time.

similar_games.title: Kingdom: New Lands
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the 2D side-view structure, forward expansion across a map, spending limited resources on defenses and units, and a run-based strategic campaign cadence.

similar_games.title: Thronefall
similar_games.why_similar: Blends simplified base building with direct on-field participation, asking you to balance economy, defenses, troop support, and personal combat in short strategic battles.

similar_games.title: Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord
similar_games.why_similar: Similar warband-management fantasy: recruit troops, upgrade forces, move across a campaign layer, and fight as an active frontline commander rather than a detached strategist.

similar_games.title: Bad North
similar_games.why_similar: Another tactics-focused game about limited-force positioning, defending and recapturing settlements, and making high-value unit composition decisions across a run-based map progression.

title: American Truck Simulator - Colorado
anchor_summary: Map expansion DLC for American Truck Simulator focused on the same long-haul trucking sim loop: accepting freight jobs, driving realistic routes, and expanding the drivable road network with new industries, cities, and scenic roads.

similar_games.title: American Truck Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: Colorado is additive DLC for this base trucking sim and keeps its exact cargo-hauling, road-driving, and company-building gameplay.

similar_games.title: Euro Truck Simulator 2
similar_games.why_similar: Same SCS long-haul truck sim structure with job delivery, realistic road driving, vehicle management, and map-based progression.

similar_games.title: American Truck Simulator - Washington
similar_games.why_similar: Another ATS state expansion adding new roads, cities, industries, and delivery routes to the same core sim.

similar_games.title: American Truck Simulator - Oregon
similar_games.why_similar: State DLC for the same game with identical freight-hauling gameplay, progression systems, and exploration-driven map expansion.

similar_games.title: American Truck Simulator - Texas
similar_games.why_similar: Large ATS map expansion built around the same realistic trucking loop, route planning, and cargo delivery across a new region.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: This title is clearly additive DLC for American Truck Simulator, and its own description focuses on new Colorado roads, cities, landmarks, industries, and cargo routes rather than a different gameplay mode. Parent-game evidence is strong: players drive licensed American trucks, deliver cargo across open road networks, obey traffic rules, manage transmissions/brakes, park long trailers, and grow from driver-for-hire into an owner-operator who expands a trucking company with garages, fleets, and hired drivers. The Colorado DLC preserves that same core loop by adding a new region to explore and haul freight through. That strongly supports transport_sim with open-world road traversal, vehicle control, sim-realism challenge, non-combat play, and company/base growth progression.

similar_games.title: Please Be Happy
similar_games.why_similar: Character-focused visual novel with strong emotional writing, relationship routes, and a gentle slice-of-life reading loop.

similar_games.title: Highway Blossoms
similar_games.why_similar: Recognizable yuri visual novel comp driven by dialogue, romance, and route-like story progression in a mostly linear interactive format.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Although this is clearly DLC and no parent-game record is supplied, the stored DLC text still gives meaningful gameplay signals: it adds a new warband with 7 unit types, a fully playable campaign, explicit weapon loadouts, skills, spells, counter-attacks, area effects, resistance stats, fear/terror tests, and melee/spell interactions. Combined with stored genre labels of Strategy and RPG and the prior audit signals for tactical tempo and tactical optimization, this most strongly fits a squad-focused turn-based tactics game with RPG progression elements. Some fingerprint fields such as perspective and exact presentation are inferred less directly than ideal, so confidence is moderate rather than high.
